rows.title: Preventing Child Trafficking: Improving Schools in Nepal
rows.description: To improve access to education by improving and extending school buildings and providing safe water supplies and sanitation facilities to schools. This will encourage school attendance in a district of low educational attainment and high child trafficking.

rows.title: Enabling local medical staff to provide basic ear care to prevent and treat needless hearing loss
rows.description: To provide local access to basic health services (ear care); to enable medical staff already working in the area to deliver basic health care services (ear care) to more people or across a wider geographic area; to extend existing health service facilities, including clinics and hospitals (by improving the audiology facility at our primary ear care centre).

rows.title: Improved Healthcare for Women in Nepal
rows.description: To improve the quality of health care, safety and hygiene for female leprosy and maternity patients by building a new ward

rows.title: Reducing earthquake risks in schools through preparedness and retrofitting
rows.description: To reduce vulnerability of children and communities to earthquake risk in 5 schools of Rupandehi district through preparedness and retrofitting measures.

rows.title: 2013 Stars Impact Awards
rows.description:
The Stars Impact Awards recognise strong locally registered organisations working to improve the lives of disadvantaged children. The 2013 Awards were provided across two regions: Africa-Middle East and Asia-Pacific. The Awards are open to organisations based in countries with the highest rates of under-five mortality. More information on eligibility can be accessed through: http://www.starsfoundation.org.uk/impact-awards/how-to-apply
In total, 15 Awards were provided in 2013 split across the two regions and four thematic categories: Health, Education, Protection and WASH (water, sanitation and hygiene).
The main Impact Award winners per region receive US$100,000 of unrestricted funding together with a bespoke package of consultancy, PR support, and media training. Runners Up will receive up to US$50,000 of unrestricted funding in addition to consultancy support. Rising Stars receive US$15,000 of unrestricted funding in addition to consultancy support.

rows.title: WW PWC Girls' Education Challenge 2B (GBP)
rows.description: Girls’ Education Challenge is a flagship FCDO multi-country programme to deliver measurable learning outcomes for the 1.5 million children.

rows.title: DFID Climate Smart Development for Nepal: Lot 1 Nepal Climate Change Support Programme (NCCSP)
rows.description: NCCSP2 aims to increase the resilience of vulnerable communities to climate change. It provides strategic support to the Govt. of Nepal to design and implement Local Adaptation Plans of Action. This will improve resilience of 700,000 poor and vulnerable people (especially women) to floods, landslides, and droughts in the most remote districts of Nepal.

rows.title: DFID Post-Earthquake Reconstruction in Nepal - Building Back Better
rows.description: Establish partnerships with local & central government, communities and businesses to support the (i) districts effected by the Earthquake to “build back better” including leading to more resilient (including climate resilient) infrastructure and institutions; (ii) the most vulnerable recover their livelihoods and assets; and (iii) the Government of Nepal to plan for and manage the response to the earthquake.

rows.title: WW PWC Girls' Education Challenge 2A (GBP)
rows.description: Girls’ Education Challenge is a flagship FCDO multi-country programme to deliver measurable learning outcomes for the 1.5 million children.

rows.reporting: Defence for Children International Netherlands - ECPAT Netherlands
rows.title: Down to Zero / Voice for Change Programme - ECPAT International
rows.description: Children and youth, both victims and at risk of CSEC, are the main actors in this intervention. We define children as girls and boys up to 18 years old and youth as adolescent girls and boys aged 18 to 24 years old. We will mostly work with them through children and youth groups. We will also work with government officials at local and national level, representing various Ministries responsible for the effective implementation of the national child protection system. We will also work with law enforcement agency officers and civil society organisations. Civil society organisations will include those providing services to CSEC victims and those at risk, organisations supporting CSEC prevention work and those working with youth as advocates.

rows.reporting: DEPARTMENT FOR BUSINESS, ENERGY & INDUSTRIAL STRATEGY
rows.title: Determinants of health in rural Nepal: Utilising PHASE Nepal data to investigate social inequalities in health and healthcare amongst under-5s
rows.description: The Global Challenges Research Fund (GCRF) supports cutting-edge research to address challenges faced by developing countries. The fund addresses the UN sustainable development goals. It aims to maximise the impact of research and innovation to improve lives and opportunity in the developing world.

rows.title: AC-CEIS Management Cost
rows.description: This project essentially covers the management cost of the projects, NPLA73 and NPLA66 under PIP, Advocacy, Campaigns, & Critical Enabling Issues Support  [ P00315]. Management cost includes the salaries, medical allowances, travel cost, communication/workshop/ meeting and logistics

rows.title: WE CAN END ALL VIOLENCE AGAINST WOMEN IN NEPAL
rows.description: In line with the SA Regional We Can End ALL Violence Against Women campaign, the project aims to reduce social acceptance of VAW in Nepal.  The work will involve working with change makers, alliances and Core Working Group towards creating fundamental shift in women's and men's attitudes, beliefs and behaviours that perpetuate gender - based violence in individuals, families, communities and state in Nepal.

rows.title: Disaster Risk Reduction and Humanitarian Project (DRR/HP)
rows.description: This project aims to ensure, women, poor and small farmers and socially excluded people in the Terai areas of Nepal, are less vulnerable to disasters and have improved livelihoods through better land and water management practices, appropriate disaster management and market access development.between women and men. The Project works with 7 partners targeting 7000 vulnerable families in 40 communities of four target districts (Rautahat, Sarlahi, Saptari and Nawalparasi) and Kathmandu valley.

rows.title: RHV_Promoting Poor & Marginalised Women's Participation in Governance in Nepal
rows.description: The project will work towards promoting equal rights of women in decision -making bodies and in ending violence against women by building their capacity to engage effectively in governance at all levels. Partner organisations will work to ensure that public policy and decision making reflect the interests of poor and marginalised women, especially those excluded from political, social, and economic life.

rows.title: Water and Sanitation Support to Vulnerable Communities in Mid West Region, Nepal
rows.description: The  project aims to reduce the incidence of water, sanitation and hygiene related diseases in conflict affected and vulnerable communities through provision of safe drinking water and improved hygiene/sanitation in Dailekh and Surkhet Districts of the Mid Western Region, Nepal. Specific objective is to improve access to safe drinking water and sanitation facilities and knowledge and practice of safe hygiene behaviours in the target communities.

rows.title: Building Community Resilience to Recurrent Floods in the Terai Region of Nepal
rows.description: This project aims to reduce the vulnerability of poor and marginalized communities (including dalits) in flood prone districts of the Terai Region of Nepal. The project shall be implemented in the 28 communities of Saptari, Sarlahi and Nawalparasi districts at local level while there will be national level advocacy, coordination and networking. The project shall directly benefit 21,407 people from community, schools, government and non -government actors in disaster risk reduction. This project, with learning from previous DIPECHO/regular DRR projects, focuses on local disaster management components, IEC, small -scale mitigation and advocacy.

rows.title: Climate Change Advocacy and Adaptation Project in Nepal
rows.description: The project aims to enable communities to adapt to climate change by supporting farmers with climate change resistant vegetable seeds, building model checkdams for management of catchments to enhance water recharge in natural springs and by influencing inclusion of community based adaptation in national and district plans and programmes. The project will build the capacity of Oxfam and partner staff, community members and district line agencies regarding climate change adaptation. It will build advocacy alliance to influence climate change policies, plans and programmes.

rows.title: Improving Food Security in Communities Vulnerable to Food Price Volatility
rows.description: This project  represents funding from the EU Food Facility. A separate project represents the co -financing elements (Top Project). The combined project addresses food security in chronically food insecure areas through social safety net support, increasing agricultural productivity through increased access to seeds and small -scale irrigation and increased access to technical support. These activities also support national advocacy  work targeting the development of long term national food security strategies.

rows.title: Water and Sanitation Support for Landslides/Flood Affected Families  in Baitadi
rows.description: The  project aims to reduce the incidence of water, sanitation and hygiene related diseases in landslides/ flood  affected communities in Baitadi District through provision of safe drinking water and improved hygiene/sanitation. Specific objective is to improve access to safe drinking water and sanitation facilities and knowledge and practice of safe hygiene behaviours in the target communities.

rows.title: LMC SPA 2022-2023
rows.description: LMC SPA 2022-2025

rows.title: RURAL LIVELIHOOD SUPPORT PROGRAMME
rows.description: The project's aims to reduce Food Insecurity and improve rural livelihood  through provision of food, seed, seed multiplication, micro -irrigation, livestocks, &  capacity strengthening. The project results will also directly contribute to the programme outputs, which are increased access to seed, irrigation water and improved awareness and advocacy on food rights and strengthened community capacity to maintain community assets and analytical capacity through Participatory Learning Centres. This project covers the budget of Oxfam contribution for EC Food Facility Project  and other Rural Livelihood Programme from different funding sources.

rows.title: Establishing Economic Rights of Rural Poor & Women through Vegetable Seed Enterp
rows.description: The vision of the Project is a thriving women farmer led business that produces and markets high quality vegetable seeds for local and regional markets. This will be achieved by supporting the existing Pabitra Janakalyan Agricultural Cooperative from Surkhet District to develop and execute a focused marketing strategy for seeds and provide technical support to it's members who are largely poor and an anticipated 3 fold small scale subsistence farmers to increase the quality and quantity of their produce.

rows.title: WASH Humanitarian Capacity Improvement Project
rows.description: This project covers the Water Sanitation and Hygiene Promotion (WASH) humanitarian capacity improvement in Four Terai Districts and four Mid and Far Western Hilly Districts and 67 VDCs within these districts, which is designed incorporating lessons learnt from our humanitarian responses (flood and diarrhoea) in 2007, 2008 and 2009 and WASH HCI project June 2010–March 2011. It is mainly focused on enhancing humanitarian capacity of district and national WASH cluster members to prepare for and respond to flood/landslides disasters and diarrhoea outbreaks (including staff of Oxfam, Government line agencies and partners) in target VDCs.

rows.title: ESSENTIAL SERVICES  CAMPAIGN IN NEPAL
rows.description: The project aims to improve awareness and understanding among the main stakeholders including staff, and users of health facilities, water and sanitation user groups, policy makers and civil society organisations on the main issues regarding delivery of Free Health Services (FHS), and free maternity health care to the poor and marginalised groups. The project will contribute to increasing capacity of civil society groups to enable them to advocate for, monitor and support the implementation of FHS, and water and sanitation policies so as to make sure that free health and watsan policies and programmes benefit the poor and marginalised groups.

rows.title: Women Empowerment Progremme in Nepal
rows.description: The main thrust of the project lies in political and economic empowerment of poor rural women throughout Oxfam's country programme. The strategy is to develop their skills through women's discussion classes referred to as Women's Empowerment Centres (WECs) that are based on REFLECT approach. The women leaders will demonstrate their leadership skills within local community decision -making structures such as Community Forestry User Groups, School Management Committees, Sub/Health Posts management Committees, Water and Sanitation User Groups, village to national level disaster management committees, cooperatives etc.

rows.title: Public Health and Basic Livelihoods Support Programme - PHABLeS  Management Cost
rows.description: Public Health and Basic Livelihoods Support Programme  - PHABLeS  in Nepal  is one of the key programme. This project covers the management and support costs of 01 April 2011 to 31 March 2012 fiscal year. This project will provide the necessary staffing and programme management funds to enable the programme to achieve the results with partnership of national and local partners in 5 districts of Nepal.  Programme support costs include the salaries of programme staff, finance staff, communication, trainings, monitoring, exposure visits and logistics.

rows.title: Food Security and Land Rights Campaign
rows.description: Fighting against poverty and suffering through land rights campaign for landless tillers will be focussed in mid and far western region,Nepal. The partner Community Self -reliance Centre (CSRC) works as network with community based organisations to access to  landless tillers at the grass root levels and policy level, so that they are able to influence policies, practices and decisions affecting their lives.  RtF National Network works for policy advocacy to the government and development agencies on food security. It raises the issues from local level to national level and linked with the government policy on food security.

rows.title: Water and Sanitation Support in Vulnerable Communitties in Darchula District
rows.description: The project focuses on responding water and sanitation needs of vulnerable communities in Darchula District  with the aim to reduce the incidence of water, sanitation and hygiene related diseases through provision of safe drinking water and sanitation facilities, and improved hygiene/sanitation practices.

rows.title: Rural Water Sanitation & Hygiene Support Programme ( Rural- WASH)
rows.description: The project focuses on responding water and sanitation needs of vulnerable communities in five Districts ( Surkhet, Dailekh, Dadeldhura, Baitadi and Darchula) of PHABLeS programme with the aim to reduce the incidence of water, sanitation and hygiene related diseases through provision of safe drinking water and sanitation facilities, and improved hygiene/sanitation practices.

rows.title: Reducing Social Acceptance of Violence Against Women in Nepal
rows.description: This project is developed on the basis of the learnings of We Can campaign and will be implemented through the two self emerged We Can networks in the western and central regions. The project will be implemented in eight Village Development Committees (VDCs) of four districts in the western and central regions. The project is aimed at creating an enabling environment for effective participation of women at the community level in influencing local institutions and society to reduce social acceptance of VAW in close coordination with local government bodies such as VDCs and other local institutions .

rows.title: My Rights, My Voice: Engaging children and youth in their rights to health
rows.description: The project aims to improve children, youth and young mothers' right to health through good quality free health services. It will contribute to increasing capacity of the target groups to identify their needs, influence decisions affecting them and advocate for the implementation of existing free health policies and specific children, youth and young mothers' health needs they identify. It will also support to build collaboration between right holders and duty bearers to interact the right holders' concerns related to health and work collectively to address them.

rows.title: Enhancing response readiness capacity of health system for major earthquake
rows.description: The specific objective of the project is to increase readiness and response capacity of health authorities, health institutions and community for an earthquake in Kathmandu District.

rows.title: Disaster Risk Management and Humanitarian Preparedness Project, Nepal
rows.description: The project, core programme of the PIP P00709 is focussed on reducing poverty and vulnerability of disaster prone groups. It has three major interventions in line with JCS; livelihood integrated community based disaster risk management and climate change adaptation, humanitarian capacity building and humanitarian advocacy. The project covers 24 VDCs in 4 districts.  The current focus will be on federating communities in VDC level cooperative and gradually transferring the role of partners to the local institutions on DRM and CCA.

rows.title: Mainstreaming disaster risk reduction (DRR) and enhancing response capacity
rows.description: The project will be implemented by a consortium of UNDP -UNOCHA -OXFAM through local partner in district (Saptari, Nawalparasi and Dadeldhura) and 2 Municipality of Kathmandu Valley. The project is funded by European  Commission for Humanitarian Aid Department, under its 6th Action plan in South Asia.

rows.title: WASH Humanitarian Capacity Improvement Project
rows.description: The project focuses on reducing vulnerability of disaster prone groups through WASH preparedness at community and household level. It covers 24 VDCs  of  4 flood prone districts in line collaboration with NPLA86.  The current focus is on preparing emergency WASH facilities as well as capacity building of community and stakeholders on emergency WASH preparedness and response.

rows.title: Strengthening participation and influence of poor and vulnerable farmers ...
rows.description: The project worked with poor and vulnerable farmers of nine Village Development Committees (VDCs) in three districts to build their knowledge, skills and capacities and network them in 90 farmer groups and networks at community and VDC level.

rows.title: ENTERPRISE DEVELOPMENT PROGRAMME DAFACOS
rows.description: - Increase the production of vegetable seeds and fresh vegetables by involving larger number of farmers as well as by improving the yield.   -

rows.title: Women Leadership Programme in Nepal
rows.description: To contribute to improving local governance through direct participation  of poor and marginalized women, especially Dalits and ethnic minorities, in the governance processes

rows.title: Urban WASH Preparedness and CBDRR & Urban Risk Management for KTM and Terai
rows.description: In line with PIP P00709, the project is centred on reducing suffering and loss of life following a major earthquake in Nepal and increased resilience of 2 municipalities to disaster risk in Nepal. The project covers 14 wards/2 Internally Displaced Person (IDP) camp sites in Kathmandu Valley and Two Terai Municipalities in the Far Western Region of Nepal: Dhangadhi (Kailali district). Mahendranagar (Kanchanpur district) and 4 wards (communities) in these Municipalities.

rows.title: Safer Remittances  and Improved Livelihoods
rows.description: The project will attract the attention of the government, private sectors and civil society organizations toward formulating appropriate policies and developing favourable mechanism for local level pre -departure orientation to the potential migrant workers. The piloting project specifically seeks to protect the migrants from losing the money and other pitfalls through these orientation package. The project will also provide financial literacy with an objective to utilize the remittances for improving livelihoods. The project activities and outcomes will be well documented and will be used for evidence based advocacy

rows.title: Urban Survey, Model Building constructions and Strengthen capacity  of KUKL
rows.description: In line with PIP P00709,urban survey will prepare a baseline status on Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) and Governance related to DRR of the selected 27 Municipalities.  Model building construction will be implemented in three selected municipalities (Chandrapur, Lumbani Sanskritik and Kawasoti) where Oxfam will replicate the model for Institutionalization of National Building Code Implementation from Dhangadi of Kailali District.Where as strengthening capacity of Kathmandu Upatayaka Khanipani Limited (KUKL) will support to strengthen the existing water supply facilities during earthquake emergency.

rows.title: Digital Vision Capacity Enhancement for Programme Staff of Nepal
rows.description: Digital Vison Training to Programme Colleagues, buy good quality camaras, laptops and trainging on photography and videodocumentary and devlop a documentary on Economic Leadership.

rows.title: Strengthening Action For Fostering Resilience (SAFER Nepal)
rows.description: The project will be implemented by Practical Action Lead consortium with Mercy Corps Nepal and Oxfam as consortium partner in 8 vulnerable districts of Nepal.  The project has 3 broad results as follows;  Result 1: Linkages between stakeholders from National to community level developed and strengthened. Result 2: Methodologies and approaches for replication and mainstreaming developed and adopted Result 3: Stakeholders strengthened to better support instituational and technical approaches

rows.title: Adaptation Finance Accountability Initiative in Nepal
rows.description: The project is a part of a global programme initiated by Oxfam in partnership with Overseas Development Institute (ODI) and World Resource Institute (WRI) implemented in four countries  - Uganda, Zambia, the Philippines and Nepal. The programme focused on four key areas  - mapping of national landscape of adaptation and/or resilience finance; follow the money and improve national and local transparency, accessibility and accountability; fostering learning and exchange of experiences and linking the global and locals. In Nepal, the project focused on first two areas.

rows.title: Women's Leadership Programme in Nepal
rows.description: To contribute to improving local governance through direct participation of poor and marginalized women, especially Dalits and ethnic minorities, in the governance processes, keep transparency and accountability of selected local decision making structures, advocate for women's political participation and incorporate their issues in political parties' manifestos during constituent assembly election process

rows.title: Enhancing the Health Sector Crisis Preparedness in Kathmandu Valley
rows.description: Awareness level of targeted communities on health and WASH preparedness and response mechanism i will be ncreased by 30% compared to baseline.Increase capacities of targeted health facilities  to manage MCI based on baseline and end line survey.

rows.title: Moving Towards Community Led Land and Agrarian Reform
rows.description: There is no community level mechanism or model for poor and vulnerable people to access land in Nepal. This project will develop and pilot a Community Led Land and Agrarian Reform model for accessing land by land poor and other vulnerable people in Nepal's hill areas. More than 2,500 families in 5 Village Development Committees (VDCs) of 3 districts will be well aware of joint land ownership policy including land rights issues. Among them, at least 500 will have received joint land ownership. At least 50 land poor families will have gained access. A report on status of previous land lease programme will be used to contribute and influence land lease policy of Nepal.

rows.title: Securing Food / GROW Campaign in Nepal
rows.description: The project will aim influencing policies, programmes and practices of government in favour of smallholder farmers, women and poor community and also aim at ensuring their rights and entitlement through developing a mechanism to engage with policy makers, researchers, NGOs and donors. The project will thus adopt the strategies of alliance and network building, research and policy studies, and advocacy and campaigning.

rows.title: Rural livelihood Support Programme
rows.description: The project's aims to reduce Food Insecurity and improve rural livelihood  through provision of food, seed, developemnt of community seed bank, micro -irrigation, livestocks, &  capacity strengthening for rural poor  and farmers. The project results will directly contribute to the programme outputs, which are increased access to seed, irrigation water and improved awareness and advocacy on food rights and strengthened community capacity to maintain community assets and analytical capacity through Participatory Learning Centres.

rows.title: Nawalparasi Rice Enterprise
rows.description: The project will be implemented in Nawalparasi district .The project will support the rice mill get access to working capital, providing mentoring and helping set up good procurement system. By helping farmers get access to good quality input and extension services and working capital through the set up of cooperative shops, farmers will be able to increase productivity, sell paddy to their own mill at a fair price, and earn extra income from dividend once the mill makes a good profit.

rows.title: Nepal Community Land Rights Project
rows.description: This project aims to increase land poor, women and small farmers' access to lands and promote sustainable use of such lands ultimately to improve livelihoods and food security of target groups. It plans to achieve this aim by enhancing knowledge, skills  and capacity of community members on land rights and land reform; improving participation in and effectiveness of community land governance; ensuring and increase land poor, women and small farmers' access to land (particularly fallow public and private lands) and promote their sustainable use; developing and implementing land and agrarian reform plan at community level. Daily office management of the project will be done by the Kathmandu based Land Rights Program Officer with 50% field visit. The project will be supervised by Policy and Advocacy Coordinator. The direct management in field level will be done by implementation partner, Community Self Reliance Center (CSRC). They will have one project coordinator in national level and two district officers in each working districts. The two district officers will work closely with 12 community facilitators (6 in each district).

rows.title: Livelihood and Empowerment Project
rows.description: LEP will continue its interventions and target to reach among 20,383 families directly in 41 village development committee of 6   districts; Udayapur, Makawanpur, Gorkha, Nawalparasi, Kapilbastu and Arghakhanchi. Besides, it will also contribute to the policy advocacy and campaign works around GROW/FCJ campaign in the country linking with community -based initiatives. During the year ,it will also undertake evaluation of one of partner organisations and document the learning from the project as part of knowledge management. The following intervention will be carried out by the project; 1. Empowering community and women 2. Integration of Disaster and climate change adaptation  to reduce vulnerability 3. Building livelihood of the community more resilient 4. Institutionalization of community groups and cooperatives to sustain impact

rows.title: Enterprise Development Programme in Nepal
rows.description: Goal of EDP programme is to improve the lives of smallholder farmers and women through the development of sustainable enterprises and a favourable enabling environment. The project includes the human resources and programme management cost for effective management and delivery of all Enterprise Development Programme (EDP) projects in Nepal under  PIP 906. The project cost includes full or partial salaries of 1 EDP Lead, 1 Business and Finance Officer and 2 EDP Officer and associated administrative cost. It also includes extension grant support to Pavitra (NPLA71) to support improved value addition through installation of Seed Processing Unit.

rows.title: Disaster Risk Reduction and Humanitarian Project
rows.description: The project has focused on reducing poverty and vulnerability of disaster prone groups. It has three major components - Climate Change Adaptation and livelihood integrated CBDRR (Community Base DRR);  Humanitarian Capacity Building especially emergency preparedness and response and humanitarian advocacy.  The project will cover 42 Village Development Committees of 7 districts in Eastern and Far Western flood region of Nepal.

rows.title: Strengthening the Health Sector Response Capacity in Kathmandu valley.
rows.description: The proposed action is a continuation of DIPECHO VI and VII and it focuses more on consolidating the achievements of previous interventions ensuring a greater ownership by the Government authority. The main objective of the intervention is to enhance the response capacity of health authorities, health institutions and community to provide medical service during and immediately after the disaster. The specific objective of action is to strengthen health response system to reduce the preventable mortality, morbidity and disease associated with the mass casualty incidents in Kathmandu Valley. Selection of project intervention area and beneficiaries was guided by the disaster vulnerability, existing response capacity and strategic importance in terms of setting up examples for future replication.

rows.title: Hand Washing Project in Nepal
rows.description: The pilot program will be part of the ongoing emergency WASH program and will target mothers that have been displaced by the earthquake in Kathmandu Valley. The program will be comprised of 3 phases:  PHASE 1: Formative baseline study. The formative baseline analysis will be comprised of focus group discussions, observations and a questionnaire survey. The objectives are to assess levels of hand washing with soap (HWWS) knowledge and practice among mothers as well as to understand motivators and barriers around HWWS at critical junctures.  The most appropriate communication channels for reaching mothers in a peri -urban context will also be identified.   PHASE 2: Materials development and pre -testing. The findings from phase one will feed into the development of targeted HWWS behaviour change strategy and set of promotional materials. The materials will be pre -tested with mothers to ensure they are accepted and understood, attractive, engaging, and motivating. Materials will be shared with relevant stakeholders.   PHASE 3: Implementation, Monitoring & Evaluation. The hand washing program will be implemented over a 4 week period. During this time the program will be monitored and mothers will have an opportunity to provide and receive feedback. An endline evaluation will be conducted post intervention.

rows.title: Urban Innovation Project - Urban Risk Management Project
rows.description: Earthquake preparedness

rows.title: Women's Leadership Programme (WLP)
rows.description: The project will empower poor and marginalised women through capacity building supports and build an enabling environment for them to strengthen and utilise their leadership skills so as to bring transformative change in personal, social and political spheres through a shift in the existing power relations. It will support them to increase their  participation in decision making, influence local governance and advocate for their rights through active participation in all democratic processes including national and local elections.

rows.title: Nepal Mid and Far West Flood and Landslide response 2014
rows.description: The significant damage and loss of lives and displaced population were reported from Mid and Far Western hilly and Southern plain districts from incessant rainfall during 13 -15 August 2014. Considering number of displaced population Oxfam decided to respond in three out of five worst affected districts with Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) and Emergency Food Security and Vulnerable Livelihood (EFSVL) support to the displaced population. Key interventions under WASH and EFSVL interventions include Cash for Work (CFW); Hygiene Kit distribution; distribution of livelihood inputs; public health awareness; latrine and water supply facilities in the Internally Displaced Person (IDP) camps.

rows.title: DAFACOS Vegetable and Seed Enterprise
rows.description: Promote entrepreneurship among farmers through business related trainings; Support Dadeldhura Farmers' Agriculture Cooperative (DAFACOS)  to strengthen the business, Establish link of the cooperative with banks for credit, National Agriculture Research Center (NARC) and District Agriculture Development Office (DADO) for technical assistance, govt for resources and other input providers; Support organizational development and strengthening of producer groups and cooperatives; Promote women's economic leadership through Participatory Laerning Centre (PLC) and Gender Equity & Social Inclusion (GESI) training; Collaborate with different actors for market expansion of the cooperative; Promote sustainable and climate smart agriculture technologies

rows.title: Securing Food/ GROW Campaign in Nepal
rows.description: The project will aim influencing policies, programmes and practices of government and other stakeholders in favour of smallholder farmers, women and poor community and also aim at ensuring their rights and entitlement. The project will thus adopt the strategies of alliance and network building, research and policy studies, and advocacy and campaigning

rows.title: Management Cost for SLFS Programme
rows.description: Food and Economic Justice programme is one of the three programme pillars of Oxfam in Nepal. This project covers the management and support costs of 01 April 2011 to 31 March 2012 fiscal year. This project will provide the necessary staffing and programme management funds to enable the programme to achieve the results with partnership of national and local partners in 12 districts of Nepal.  Programme support costs include the salaries of programme staff, finance staff, communication, training, monitoring, exposure visits and logistics.

rows.title: Disaster Risk Reduction and Humanitarian Preparedness
rows.description: The project focuses on reducing poverty and vulnerability of disaster prone groups. It has three major components - enhancing community resiliency through DRR and Climate Change Adaptation, Mainstreaming DRR into policies, plans and budget and gender sensitive institutional capacity building of government private sectors and Civil Society Organisations. The project covers 24 Village Development Committees of four districts Sarlahi, Saptari, Rautahat and Dhanusha where Oxfam in Nepal has been implementing DRR -Humanitarian Programme in the past.

rows.title: WASH Innovation Nepal
rows.description: A model of multiple use system (MUS) and integrated water resource management (IWRM) will be demonstrated during implementation of the project. Around 8.7 KW of hydro electricity will be generated and will provide electricity to 25 households of Jayathala village using available head of 135 m and 11 lps of water from Kachulighad, a perennial stream.  The intake and canal of the existing irrigation system will be upgraded for additional discharge of 11 lps, required for hydro plant. Fore bay (for sedimentation), turbine house and transmission line will be constructed. The generated electricity will be distributed to the individual household. 350 watt will be allocated for individual house. Efficient lighting techniques will be used like CFL and LED. In the present context, 50 watt is sufficient for the lighting in a house. Remaining 300 watts (for each household) will be used for cooking and other available electronic appliances. To reduce the deforestation, project will support the electric cooking stoves to each household. The behavioural change on shifting firewood to the electrical appliances for cooking will be monitor closely. Further, the electrical appliance (cooking stoves) will provide opportunity for boiling the drinking water.    This Mini hydro will create ample opportunities in the village like small size workshops, small mills etc to flourish, and dryer (agricultural production) and promote end use of the energy generated (especially of day time). This will certainly create better employment and entrepreneurship opportunities.

rows.title: Earthquake Response and Recovery for Dhading District affected Communites
rows.description: The 3 years project implemented in 5 village Development Committees (with selected activities in other 3 VDCs) of Dhading  district  aims at contributing to a gender responsive sustainable recovery and reducing risk among 43000 vulnerable affected women (at least 52%) men, boys, girls, elderly . To this aim, the project includes multisector emergency response, increase ability of communities to sustain gender sensitive WASH services, to sustain livelihoods despite of shocks and to access resources for resilient shelter. It will also contribute to increasing capacity of the target groups/committees to identify their needs & opportunities, influence decisions affecting them, communities mobilization, awareness on safe Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) practices, and advocate for the access on the resources & services for recovery & reconstruction by March 2018. The project also aims at influencing government  practices of services delivery for improved gender responsive assistance. Safe and equitable access for women and men to support is a key outcome of the project.

rows.title: Earthquake Response and Recovery for Kathmandu Valley affected Communities
rows.description: The four years project implemented in 11 municipalities  and three Village Development Committees (VDCs) of three districts in Kathmandu valley aims at contributing to a gender responsive sustainable recovery and reducing risk amongst 150,381 vulnerable affected women (at least 50%) and men. To this aim, the project includes multi sector emergency response, increase ability of communities to sustain gender sensitive Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) services, to sustain livelihoods despite of shocks and to access resources for resilient shelter along with the disaster preparedness interventions in the selected municipalities. It will also contribute to increasing capacity of the target groups/committees to identify their needs and opportunities, influence decisions affecting them, community mobilization, awareness on safe Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) practices, and advocate for the access on the resources and services for recovery and reconstruction by March 2018. The project also aims at influencing government practices of services delivery for improved gender responsive assistance. Safe and equitable access for women and men to support is a key outcome of the project. Also, the project aims disaster preparedness intervention in municipalities for improved emergency preparedness and response capacity at municipal and ward level.

rows.title: Earthquake Response and Recovery for Nuwakot District affected Communites
rows.description: The 4 years project implemented in 10 Village Development Communities (VDCs) of Nuwakot  district  aims at contributing to a gender responsive sustainable recovery and reducing risk amongst 73000 vulnerable affected women (at least 70%) and men. To this aim, the project includes multisector emergency response, increase ability of communities to sustain gender sensitive WASH services, to sustain livelihoods despite of shocks and to access resources for resilient shelter. It will also contribute to increasing capacity of the target groups/committees to identify their needs & opportunities, influence decisions affecting them, communities mobilization, awareness on safe Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) practices, and advocate for the access on the resources & services for recovery & reconstruction by March 2018. The project also aims at influencing government  practices of services delivery for improved gender responsive assistance. Safe and equitable access for women and men to support is a key outcome of the project.

rows.title: Gender and Protection Programme in Earthquake Affected Districts of Nepal
rows.description: Support existing community based women's rights groups/organizations to design and implement interventions for the most vulnerable and marginalized groups in Earthquake affected OXFAM's working districts. Women, Dalits and other socially marginalised ethnic groups, who are among the poorest, are particularly vulnerable and may not have equitable access to assistance provided in recovery and reconstruction. Considering the deeply ingrained social exclusion of some groups, donors, aid agencies and the government must adopt inclusive and safe programming approaches, make conscious and concerted efforts to consult vulnerable groups, address their specific needs and issues considering their equitable access to recovery and reconstruction.

rows.title: Creating space to take action on violence against women and girls
rows.description: Creating Spaces is a five -year program which aims to reduce violence against women and girls, including the prevalence of early and forced marriage, through coordinated prevention and response initiatives in Bangladesh, India, Indonesia, Nepal, Pakistan and Philippines. The program will take an integrated and multi -faceted approach, acting on multiple levels (individual, community, institutional and societal) with diverse actors.

rows.title: Strengthening women's political participation and leadership
rows.description: Phase I (2017) Women's Leadership Programme (WLP) is ending in March 2017 but this proposed project will address the gaps and will function as the bridging phase for an inclusion of more women's political empowerment initiatives. With the advent of the local and provincial elections, the project will build on the unique opportunity of support aspiring women leaders to run for the elections. An approximate number of 450 community women leaders from 10 project Village Development Committees (VDCs) in Surkhet and Makwanpur districts will be supported through various activities to enhance their capacity, skill and knowledge to take part in the elections.  Phase II (May 2018 -April 2019): Considering the timeliness and the demand generated from the previous project of Oxfam in Nepal and Oxfam America "Strengthening women's political participation and leadership in 2017 local elections in Nepal (May 2017 - March 2018)", this project aims to strengthen and build the capacity of newly elected women leaders at two levels. Firstly, the project intends to build the capacity of all 37 women legislative members of Province 3 to enhance their leadership skills particularly on public speaking, knowledge on parliamentary processes, planning and budgeting, and advocacy and networking. Secondly, the project aims to build the capacity of selected 28 newly elected Local Women Leaders of Province 3 to develop their knowledge and skills on the concept, structure, policies, processes, planning, implementation and monitoring practices of local government, Transformative Leadership for Women's Rights (TLWRs) and interrelation of province and local government.    This project will focus to build the capacity of 37 elected legislative women members of  Province 3 and 28 elected women leaders at local level in Oxfam Nepal Project areas in Province 3 Makwanpur. The project will cover the 28 locally elected Women Leaders from Oxfam and WOSCC project areas in Makwanpur districts..

rows.title: Building Resilience and Climate Adaptive Planning in Urban Areas of Nepal
rows.description: Major intervention of this project would be  - vulnerable urban centers imbibe climate adaptation and resilience  - private sectors develop adaptation friendly policy, practice and business plans  - vulnerable urban dwellers equipped with information, resources and appropriate technology to respond to and recover from climate extremes and disaster  - knowledge documentation and dissemination

rows.title: Women's Leadership Programme (WLP)
rows.description: Outline Description: What would you like to do? The project will empower and build the capacity of poor and marginalised women and support them to utilise their leadership skills so as to bring transformative change in personal, social and political spheres through a shift in the existing power relations. It will support them to increase their  participation in decision making, influence local governance and advocate for their rights through active participation in all democratic processes. The project covering Village Development Committees (VDCs) of two districts -Surkhet and Makwanpur - - works at the central level also, specifically for policy influencing.

rows.title: Livelihood and Empowerment Project (LEP)
rows.description: Livelihood and Empowerment Project (LEP) targets to reach among 10,684  members of 30 cooperative , through cooperative strengthening  development and campaigning activities ,directly in 30  village development committee of 5 districts; Udayapur, Makawanpur, Nawalparasi, Kapilbastu and Arghakhanchi. Besides, it will also contribute to the policy advocacy and campaign works around GROW/Food  & Economic Justice campaign in the country linking with community -based initiatives. It will also undertake phase review of LEP and document the outcomes and lesson learned from the project as part of knowledge management and .the phase review document help Oxfam to design new phase of the project in Nepal. The following intervention will be carried out by the project; 1.Women and Community Empowerment 2. Integration of Disaster and climate change adaptation  to reduce vulnerability 3. Building livelihood of the community more resilient 4. Institutionalization and strenthening of community groups and cooperatives to sustain impact 5. Influencing pro -poor policy on food, agriculture, land and climate change; by linking community work with policy influencing with GROW/FEJ  campaign

rows.title: Securing Food/ GROW Campaign in Nepal
rows.description: The project will aim influencing policies, programmes and practices of government and other stakeholders in favour of smallholder farmers, women and poor community and also aim at ensuring their rights and entitlement. The project will thus adopt the strategies of alliance and network building, research and policy studies, and advocacy and campaigning

rows.title: FEJ Managment cost
rows.description: Food and Economic Justice (FEJ) Programme is one of the three programme pillars of Oxfam in Nepal. This project covers the management and support costs of 01 April 2016 to 31 March 2020 fiscal year. This project will provide the necessary staffing and programme management funds to enable the programme to achieve the results with partnership of national and local partners in 12 districts of Nepal.  Programme support costs include the salaries of programme staff, finance staff, communication, training, monitoring, exposure visits and logistics.

rows.title: Rural Livelihood Support Programme in Nepal
rows.description: The project's aim to improve food security and livelihoods status of poor, socially excluded and vulnerable women and men through provision of food, seed, micro -irrigation,  enterprises development, market access &  capacity strengthening of farmers groups and cooperatives. The project results will directly contribute to the programme outputs, which are increased access to seed, micro irrigation, market and advocacy on food rights, joint land ownerships, 15% Village Development Committee (VDC) budget allocation in agriculture sector and strengthened community capacity to maintain community assets and increase awareness through women empowerment centre.

rows.title: DAFACOS Vegetable and Seed Enterprise
rows.description: Objectives of this project is to expand the business by increasing the number of share members by nearly two fold from the present 601 to 1184 by the end of the fourth year, out of which the % of new women  share member will be 80% and to increase DAFACOS annual turnover of seed to 145 MT

rows.title: Nawalparasi Rice Enterprise
rows.description: The project will be implemented in Nawalparasi district .The project will suport the rice mill get access to working capital, providing mentoring and helping set up good procurement system. By helping farmers get access to good quality input and extension services and working capital through the set up of cooperative shops, farmers will be able to increase productivity, sell paddy to their own mill at a fair price, and earn extra income from dividend once the mill makes a good profit.

rows.title: Enterprise Development Programme in Nepal
rows.description: Enterprise Development Programme (EDP) is one of the priority and focus progamme elements of Oxfam's Food and Economic Justice (FEJ) programme in Nepal. This management project will provide the necessary staffing and programme management funds to enable the EDP projects to achieve the results with partnership of national and local partners, private companies, Bank & Financial Institute (BFIs) in Nepal.  Programme support costs include the salaries of programme staff, travel, communication, training, monitoring, exposure visits and networking costs. This project covers  influencing activity costs from 01 April 2016 to 31 March 2020 fiscal year. This project also covers the cost of EDP Pavitra under the contract ID R04036.

rows.title: Rural Livelihood Support Programme in Nepal
rows.description: The project's aims to improve food security and livelihoods status of poor, socially excluded and vulnerable women and men through provision of seed, micro -irrigation,  enterprises development, market access and  capacity strengthening of farmers groups and cooperatives. The project results will directly contribute to the programme outputs, which are increased access to seed, micro irrigation, market and advocacy on food rights, joint land ownerships, 15% Village Development Committee (VDC) budget allocation in agriculture sector and strengthened community capacity to maintain community assets and increase awareness through women empowerment centre.

rows.title: Urban Resilience Pilot Project
rows.description: - To reduce gender based violence and wage discrimination by organising and empowering informal sector workers.      - To promote and protect job opportunity and security   of informal sector workers by building   skills, alternative business opportunities through   networking and cooperation building.       - To reduce the vulnerability of informal sector workers by promoting social safety nets and disaster risk knowledge

rows.title: Water Supply Project for EQ Affected Population in Dhading, Nuwakot and Gorkha
rows.description: This project seeks to address water scarcity caused by the April/May 2015 earthquakes in Dhading, Gorkha and Nuwakot districts in Nepal, covering 18 Village Development Committees (VDCs).  The project will restore and improve access to sufficient, safe, sustainable and inclusive water supply, strengthen V -WASH CCs and Water User Committees (WUCs), and promote water management to sustain gender responsive WASH facilities and services reaching 44,646 beneficiaries. The intended impact will be achieved primarily through rehabilitation/new construction of a total of 52 community water systems with additional catchment protection measures where appropriate to sustain/increase recharge and reduce water source contamination. Oxfam's work and this project specifically emphasizes Institutional, functional, environmental and financial sustainability by building foundations for strong water supply governance, through enhancing capacity of D/VWASH -CCs and WUCs and placing emphasis upon water resource management and appropriate technology selection. The project includes water quality improvement through hygiene promotion related to household water treatment, storage and handling.  Oxfam's work is based upon strong gender and vulnerability/poverty analysis, and thus robustly targets vulnerable and marginalized populations. It will be delivered through partner -led implementation.

rows.title: Supporting Durable Solutions
rows.description: This project will assist displaced households and the government in developing and implementing participatory and ethical durable solutions and mitigate the immediate risks displaced households are experiencing throughout the course of the intervention in earthquake affected districts, Gorkha, Dhading, Nuwakot, Rasuwas, Sindhupalchowk and Kathmandu Valley of Nepal.

rows.title: Trans Boundary Rivers of South Asia (TROSA)- Water Governance
rows.description: Firstly the project intends to improve policies and practice of governments that protect the rights of river basin communities to water resources. Secondly, it improves policies and practice of private sector and other actors that respect the rights of river basin communities to water resources. Thirdly, it strengthens the capacity of river basin communities and civil society to participate in and influence trans -boundary water resource management. Fourthly it amplifies women's profile and influence in dialogues and decision -making on infrastructure.

rows.title: Enterprise Development Programme- R & D Innovative Solution/Vegetable
rows.description: Enterprise Development Programme (EDP) will support R&D Innovative Solutions Pvt Ltd in the establishment of smooth supply chain of vegetables, by involving 500 urban poor and small holder farmers (60% women) of Gundu, Bhaktapur in integrated farming practice. With, EDP's support, R&D's will make Agribusiness Service centre in Gundu, a hub for at least 500 urban poor farmers, supplying all necessary inputs for greenhouse cultivation, technical extension services and buy back guarantee of the produce at fair prices. EDP will support R&D to access loan from banks by providing guarantee. Thus, working capital for inputs, including greenhouses, will be funded through EDP loan. EDP will support R&D to establish Agribusiness Service Centre  -a profitable business by the end of three years and double the income of at least 500 farmers within this period and at the same time expand the sales of R&D by increasing its Green mart outlets (R&D's own vegetable outlets) and B2B Businesses.

rows.title: Enterprise Development Programme - Pokhara Women Skill Devt. Pvt. Ltd
rows.description: The project will support Pokhara Women Skill Development Pvt Ltd in increasing their production of handmade woven products, especially bags, dolls, and decorative items. PWSD works with women member producers who belong to marginalised communities and are vulnerable and economically dependent on their husbands. The project will help to create employment opportunities for 900 women producers by providing training, enhancing their capacity, increasing access to finance of PWSD through loan guarantee, and ultimately making it sustainable by increasing sales revenue. The project also aims to contribute on bringing out different women issues and reducing domestic violence through Rapid Care Analysis and Participatory Learning Centers (PLCs) , and thereby empowering women. The project will work with banks, private sectors, government and other market actors to help recognize the Fair -Trade Business Model among wider stakeholders.

rows.title: WaSH Recovery Assistance to Earthquake Affected Communities of Sindhupalchowk
rows.description: Major Intervention the working area Water supply Schemes, Water Sanitation and Hygiene (Wash) in School, Wash in Health post, Capacity building training.

rows.title: Makwanpur Livelihood Recovery Project
rows.description: The project's aims to increase the income and food security of the earthquake affected families in the target communities of Makwanpur through provision of farming inputs, tools and technical assistance to rebuild their livelihoods. The project will strengthen the capacity of local cooperatives to provide agri - inputs, financial, market and other services to the farmers and support earthquake affected women members for accessing and use of productive resources.

rows.title: Flash Flood Emergency Response in Nepal
rows.description: Purposed activities for next 2 to 3 months • Food basket support to affected HH (Food basket cost / package for 1 month ( 400 gm cereal, 60 gm pulses, 25 gm oil, 50 gm fortified blended soybean, 15 gm salt) /person /day @ 7,500/HH). • Detail livelihood assessment for next 6 months, detail livelihood and need assessment • Livelihood protection for 250 HHs, Business support grant to small shopkeepers - based on the detail need assessment and market assessment.  • Livelihood recovery for 2500 HHs, Livelihood recovery support package (seed, fertilizer, livestock) based on the detail need assessment and market assessment • Emergency Shelter Kit distribution: The primary target of this activity  is to ensure that displaced families are provided with immediate life -saving shelter solutions in the form of Emergency Shelter Kit (2 pcs of 4m x 6m tarpaulin per household, 20 m of Nylon Rope).  • Household Kits distribution: Ideally each household receiving Emergency Shelter Kit should receive one household kit containing essential non -food items so that normal household activities can resume. There are two different kinds of HH Kits used by Oxfam so far:

rows.title: Post Earthquake Reconstruction_WASH in Nepal
rows.description: Our efforts in reconstruction works will be for provision of drinking water, but our work will encourage improved sanitation and promote good hygiene practices. Improving water supplies means providing water more reliably, of better quality and closer to households, thereby reducing water -related disease and freeing time for women to undertake more productive activities and for girls to attend school. We have integrated programmes for sanitation and hygiene promotion into our activities to ensure that these wider benefits for women and the whole community are achieved and sustained.

rows.title: Safe Water for Poor Rural Communities of Southern Terai
rows.description: Improving the lives of vulnerable and marginalized families of Pidari Village and Fatuha Maheshpur in the southern Terai lowlands of Nepal by providing them with access to safe drinking water that meets the Nepal Government Standard. In the longer term (2 -3 years after receipt of safe water begins in the third year of the project), we expect to see these villages become home to healthier communities – with the number of water -based illnesses decreasing, healthier children in school, and increased productivity. After the project has finished, our local partners in the area continue to monitor communities and will be able to provide updates on their general health, in particular the number of water -related diseases being reported.

rows.title: Building Economic Resilience Project
rows.description: The project will address the needs of seasonal migrant workers and their families by increasing their access to safer remittance services in project intervention areas. The project will support six rural cooperatives to develop remittance transfer mechanism and financial products including remittance based saving and credit, insurance and utility services to meet the financial requirements of migrant families. The project will build the capacity of seasonal migrant families to start their own business

rows.title: DRR/HTN Preparedness and Enhancing CSOs roles for climate resilient communities
rows.description: The project focuses on reducing poverty and vulnerability of disaster prone communities. It has five major components  - enhancing community based disaster preparedness, Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH), Livelihood and Mainstreaming Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) into policies, plans and budget and gender sensitive institutional capacity building of government private sectors and Civil Society Organisations (CSOs). The project covers 17 wards of two districts Saptari and Rautahat where Oxfam in Nepal has been implementing Disaster Risk Reduction -Humanitarian Preparedness (DRR -HP) in the past.

rows.title: Sustainable WASH
rows.description: Oxfam will work with Birendranagar municipality, private sectors, communities, and existing Information and Communication Technology (ICT) companies to prepare waste management system for increased efficiency in solid waste management. Firstly it will strengthen the municipality's capacity in proper planning and monitoring of waste management through the use of Geo -Spatial Information System (GIS).

rows.title: Food Security, Livelihoods and Income for IDPs
rows.description: 1. Expanded skills of IDPs 2. Increased opportunities for off -farm small and micro -enterprises 3. Increased opportunities for sustainable and intensive farming 4. Improved access to government services and social networks

rows.title: Handicraft Enterprise Development Programme - Pokhara Women Skill Devt. Pvt. Ltd
rows.description: This EDP project with PWSD will provide access to loan, capacity building and increase profitability of the enterprise as well as skill development of the existing and new women member producers. It will also strengthen the networking and advocacy skills of the women producers through PLCs

rows.title: COVID-19 (PDJ, EC-CSO, OI)
rows.description: The project will ensure poor, vulnerable and marginalised people get adequate, inclusive and safe water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) support to prevent and reduce the infection risk of the COVID -19 disease outbreak. We are also supporting these vulnerable groups to be protected from the negative impacts of state emergency measures that have been implemented as part of the Covid -19 response in Nepal.

rows.title: COVID-19 (Unilever)
rows.description: Oxfam and its partners are committed to ensuring sustained improvement in hygiene and health behaviours, not only to limit the spread of Covid -19, but also with the aim of improving overall health and safety of the most vulnerable. The use and further adaptation of Mum's Magic Hands (MMH) approach to the context of Covid -19 will result in a robust understanding of behavioural motivators, and as such will be vital to promote and sustain relevant hygiene practices to reduce the spread of Covid -19. Understanding and addressing unintended negative consequences of this pandemic, particularly in relation to gender, is a key priority for Oxfam and its partners to ensure women are not disproportionately burdened, discriminated and dis empowered due to lock down and isolation.

rows.title: SCOP-R3
rows.description: Strengthening community preparedness and responses

rows.title: SIANI
rows.description: Swedish International Agricultural network Initiative (SIANI) Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) Policy Coherence Expert Group of Nepal is for collaboration that actualizes the interconnections between SDG 2 and other SDGs. The Government of Nepal is drafting a law promoting the conservation and sustainable use of agrobiodiversity. Using this drafting process as a catalyst. These the Expert Group links isolated policy processes to increase collaboration and  understanding of how the draft bill can link to nutrition and climate resilience policy and action. At the national level, using the draft bill as an entry point, the Expert Group will build understanding and collaboration that raises awareness of the importance of Small -Scale Farmers (SSF) and agricultural biodiversity focused in particular on nutrition and climate resilience. Attention to specific, identified goals for policy coherence will support the collaborative development of policy briefs on the interlinkages, a public conference and blogs. It will also contribute to the second phase of the Swedish International Development Agency (SIDA) -funded in the Oxfam Novib Sowing Diversity=Harvesting Security project (SD -HS) as it considers cross -sectoral policy connections. It works to achieve following outcomes:  Outcome 1: Increased awareness of key decision -makers, research institutes and smallholder farming communities of the: Outcome 2: Increased policy literacy and capacity of smallholder producers working with agricultural biodiversity to engage with policy -makers. Outcome 3: Expert Group contributes to integrated and coherent policies that reflect the essential links between smallholder producers, agricultural biodiversity and food and nutrition security and climate resilience.

rows.title: Covid-19 Second Wave Response-Appeal Fund
rows.description: The objective of this humanitarian project is to contribute to ensuring that poor, vulnerable and marginalized people get the adequate, inclusive, and safe support on Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) to prevent and reduce the infection risk of the COVID -19 and water borne disease outbreak in Saptan and Sarlahi districts, Nepal.

rows.title: Gender Responsive Economic Empowerment project in Nepal (GREEN)
rows.description: The project aims to contribute to climate justice, resilient livelihood, income, food security and women leadership development of farmers and seasonal migrant worker's families of Nepal.  The final beneficiaries are families of Nepali migrant workers to India in order to bring desired changes in lives and livelihoods of the migrant worker's families, the project will work with the following target groups, cooperatives.  Women Empowerment Centres (WEC) , local municipalities, district authorities and provincial government.

rows.title: "Let her voice be heard" Transformative Leadership of women Leaders in Nepal
rows.description: While the Campaign will have greater emphasis and resources placed on country level change at global level the strategic programme will also support global influencing, a strong culture of programme learning, an innovative online community of practice, components of action -research and flexible funding mechanism. Activities include compelling evidence -based research,   edutainment, social and mass media messaging, public stunts, teaching and modelling positive behaviours through art, engaging and influencing key institutional stakeholders, dialogue forums for enhancing policy framework and law enforcement, partnering and supporting platforms to escalate influence, support Women's Right Organisations (WROs') agendas in Sexual and Reproductive Health Rights (SRHR) and strengthening their capacity to raise their voices, as well as capacity building to support youth clubs for empowerment. As FECM is inextricably linked with poverty and economic status, activities will also include skills development trainings for increased economic opportunities for young women.

rows.title: Youth led GESI initiatives for COVID recovery strategies and actions in Nepal
rows.description: Project will build the human capital of youth and young women (aged 18 -40) in Karnali and Sudurpaschim province. At the same time will support in strengthening the capacity of service provider and local palika to delivery survivor centric and youth friendly services. As well as bring youth and young model as model of green business model. Total reach of project is 25,000 youths.

rows.title: People's Landscape Approach
rows.description: In the past, communities' people have their access and control over their resources within their or nearby watershed in ad hoc basis. The concept of Integrated Water Resource Management (IWRM) emerged and applied to minimize challenges to increase access and control over resources to local communities. It is also believed that bottom up planning approach of resource management was somehow practiced by application of IWRM. However, it has not been able to  understand complex relationship between human and resources available including economic, social, cultural and spiritual aspects in the watershed/landscape.  Realizing the changing complex relations of human and natural resources, the study conducted on current and past projects implemented by Oxfam indicated the need  to broaden its current approach and move beyond its combined approach of technical production support and advocacy for water and rights. Oxfam in Nepal and its partners (Community Self Relience Centre (CSRC), Local Initiatives for Biodiversity, Research and Development (LIBIRD) , Nepal Environment and Education Development Society (NEEDS)  and International Centre for Integrated Mountain Development (ICIMOD ) and Oxfam Novib team with the support of Wageningen Centre for Development Innovation (WCDI) developed People's Landscape Approach (PLA) Framework. The framework provides new opportunities to conceptualize the complex relations between humans and nature, calling for more integrated forms of conservation and natural resources management, with the aim to reconcile conservation and development objectives within a single space.  In the fiscal year 2022 -2023, Oxfam in Nepal and its partners have planned to pilot the PLA framework in Khalla Mussiti and Rangoon watersheds (landscape ) of Mahakali River basin in Kanchanpur and Dadeldhura districts of Nepal respectively.

rows.title: Partners' Investment Fund (PIF)
rows.description: - Agree the process and operational mechanism with existing partners   - Actions to support meeting partner's vision and strategies (mainly identified/felt by partners)  - Project will run for 3 years initially   - Steering mechanism will be developed   - Simple and flexible reporting and reflection practice

rows.title: Developing Alternative Management Model (AMM) for Rural Water Supply Schemes
rows.description: To test the Water Supply Management Board model at palika level that will benefit local community and government in increased access to safe, affordable, reliable and sustainable water supply through increased private sector engagement in public service delivery.

rows.title: Building Economic Resilience in Seasonal Migrant Families
rows.description: The project will increase economic participation of women from seasonal migrant families while engaging men/boys in reducing Violence Against Women and Girls (VAWG). The project will build knowledge of 250 women in financial literacy, establish 10 women led business and build vocational capacity of 21 women.

rows.title: DRR/HTN Preparedness and Enhancing CSOs roles for climate resilient communities
rows.description: The project focuses on reducing poverty and vulnerability of disaster prone communities. It has five major components  - enhancing community based disaster preparedness, Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH), Livelihood and Mainstreaming Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) into policies, plans and budget and gender sensitive institutional capacity building of government private sectors and Civil Society Organisations (CSOs). The project covers 17 wards of two districts Saptari and Rautahat where Oxfam in Nepal has been implementing Disaster Risk Reduction -Humanitarian Preparedness (DRR -HP) in the past.

rows.title: Climate Change Adaptation and Advocacy Project in Nepal
rows.description: Over the period of 38 months, the proposed project will work with active Community Based Organisations (CBOs) and Civil Society Organisations (CSOs), that were formed and capacitated by the existing projects, such as women empowerment centre (WEC), farmer's groups, community forestry user's groups (CFUG), community disaster management committees (CDMCs), youths' groups etc. Through these groups, we will reach the most marginalized, climate affected poor, excluded and women groups. To meet the long -term development objective, the project will focus its field level climate change action works to one municipality in Rautahat district i.e., Chandrapur municipality. The community level climate action works will be captured as evidence for provincial and federal level influencing and advocacy. The evidence generation and advocacy works can be done side by side however it takes some time to collect evidence and do advocacy and influencing works. The target for influencing work is local government, provincial government, federal government and international stakeholders as well. Over the period of 38 months, the project will directly reach to 5,000 people from 1,000 House Holds (HHs) through 10 CBOs/CSOs. Out of 10 CBOs/CSOs, at least 2 will be women's organizations such as WEC or women's groups.

rows.title: Watershed Project
rows.description: From the project, Oxfam would like to contribute to develop Water governance structure at watershed level being the secretariat of Government of Nepal.

rows.title: YG Entertainment
rows.description: Objective 1: Creating opportunities for young boys and girls to learn and engage in indigenous music, art and dances.

rows.title: Cultivating Change Programme
rows.description: The world is facing multiple, connected, and worsening crises. Unjust and unsustainable food systems are failing to deliver food and nutrition security, living incomes and decent working conditions for increasing numbers of people. Extractive and unsustainable use and governance of land and other natural resources are contributing to pollution, biodiversity loss, ecological breakdown, and climate change – threatening the survival of all of humanity. The impacts of these crises are profoundly unequal – rural women and communities in many countries across Africa, Asia and Latin America are disproportionately affected. Cultivating Change is a strategic program of work on food, land and natural resources, and climate. In this program we work with the rural women and communities most affected by worsening food insecurity, exploitation of natural resources and the climate crisis to challenge and transform the systems of inequality and rights abuses that are causing hunger, poverty and climate and ecological breakdown. This overarching programmatic framework (also described in this document as an umbrella program framework) has been developed to build on Oxfam's decades -long experience of working with rural communities and allies from local to global levels to fight the food, land, biodiversity and climate crises, including the Swedish International Development Agency (SIDA) -financed Seeds  - Oxfam food Justice Campaign (GROW) program (2013 -2023). The framework has been co -created through consultation with Oxfam and partner colleagues from multiple countries, representing a diverse range of technical perspectives and experience on the ground.

rows.title: Valuing Women's Work
rows.description: This project will support informal workers including domestic workers and unpaid care workers in collectively claiming their rights and protection. Also, this project will also support in shifting narratives on defining and valuing women's work.

rows.title: Jajarkot Earthquake Response Programme
rows.description: Immediate response and long term rehabilitation and development of Earthquake (EQ) affected communities of Jajarkot and adjoining districts. The immediate response will be provided on Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH), Shelter and Emergency Food Security and Vulnerable Livelihood (EFSVL) with gender and protection as cross -cutting issues. The long -term focus will be on integrated settlement/village development for affected communities.

rows.title: EMPOWERING HER: Advancing Women's Economic and Political Leadership
rows.description: Empowering Her: Advancing Women's Economic and Political Leadership for Climate Resilient Communities is one of the strategic and integrated projects of Oxfam that builds on two thematic areas (i) Gender justice, (ii) Resilience and climate (economic) justice of Oxfam in Nepal (OIN). The objective of the project is to increase economic resilience of women through participation in climate resilient and sustainable economic activity and (ii) Strengthen women social capacity (leadership and reduce Gender Based Violence (GBV)  in climate affected area.

rows.title: Women Rights Fund (WRF)

rows.title: Building Community Resilience to Climate Risks and Vulnerabilities (BCRC)

rows.title: Co-DiRECT Nepal: a community-based diet programme for remission of type 2 diabetes and amelioration of non-communicable disease risks
rows.description:
1. To establish medium/long-term effectiveness and effect sizes (T2D remissions) from a  proven intervention, culturally adapted for the rapidly growing, underserved, city populations in Nepal, a new low- and middle-income country (LMIC) setting.
2. To record and evaluate participant and community experiences of T2D remission 
implementation, defining barriers, incentives and need for further cultural adaptions.
3. To explore and optimise the programme implementation about cultural context, local  leadership, and the most appropriate approaches to evaluation and audit.
4. To seek opportunities for facilitation to improve implementation reach, scalability and  sustainability, considering staff roles, knowledge, attitudes, and skills.
5. To prepare the ground for future programme expansion: policy, practice and public 
awareness about NCD risk factors 
6. To understand wider sustainability considerations (e.g. food security, urbanisation, etc)
7. To co-create, with Nepali stakeholders, a novel evaluation-oriented framework suited to  T2D remission implementation in LMIC urban community environments, as a conceptual  project outcome.
